question:
Let $X$ denote the proportion of allotted time that a randomly selected engineer spends working on a certain project, and suppose that the probability density function of $X$ is

$$
f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{cl}
(\theta+1) x^\theta & \text { for } 0 \leq x \leq 1 \\
0 & \text { otherwise }
\end{array} .\right.
$$

where $\theta>-1$.
A random sample of 10 engineers yielded data

$$
\begin{aligned}
& x_1=0.92, x_2=0.79, x_3=0.90, x_4=0.65, x_5=0.86 \\
& x_6=0.47, x_7=0.73, x_8=0.97, x_9=0.94, x_{10}=0.77
\end{aligned}
$$

(a) Use the method of moments to obtain an estimator of $\theta$, and then compute the estimate for this data.
(b) Obtain the maximum likelihood estimator of $\theta$, and then compute the estimate for the given data.
